definition:
1. Dep., used in present and imperfect : 
2. intransitive to work for one's daily bread ; generally, to toil, work, labour , (Homer) 
3. to be poor  or needy , Solon , (Euripides), etc. 
4. with genitive to be poor in, have need of , (Aeschulus Tragicus) 
5. transitive to work at, prepare, get ready , δαῖτα πένοντο (Odyssey by Homer); τί σε χρὴ ταῦτα πένεσθα; (Odyssey by Homer) (ML)
